cookie.banner.title: A quick note about cookies
cookie.banner.description: We use technical cookies to keep the portal running and one preference cookie to remember your theme between visits. No advertising trackers.
cookie.dialog.title: Cookie preferences
cookie.dialog.description: Choose which cookies you want to enable. Technical ones are required and can't be turned off.
cookie.dialog.technical.title: Strictly technical
cookie.dialog.technical.description: Session, CSRF protection and your decision on these preferences. Without them you can't sign in.
cookie.dialog.preferences.title: Preferences
cookie.dialog.preferences.description: Remember your theme (light/dark) between visits. If you disable them, the portal still works but forgets your choice when you close the browser.
cookie.dialog.authenticated_note: You're signed in: your theme lives in your account, and the sidebar position is functional admin UI state. There are no preference cookies left to manage here. You can change your theme in Settings whenever you want.
cookie.dialog.see_more: All the detailed info lives in the

legal.footer.contact: Questions about this document? Drop us a line and we'll get back to you. You can find the contact email in the "Contact" section of each document.

dashboard.onboarding.step.gestures.label: Try gesture control

dashboard.onboarding.step.profile.label: Complete your profile
dashboard.onboarding.step.profile.descri: Add your avatar and a short bio to personalize your account.
vision.calibration.title: Set Up Facial Controls
vision.calibration.step1.title: Step 1: Enable your camera
vision.calibration.step1.desc: We need camera access to detect your facial gestures. Make sure you have good lighting.
vision.calibration.step1.denied: Camera permission denied. Enable it in your browser settings.
vision.calibration.step2.title: Step 2: Neutral expression
vision.calibration.step2.desc: Look straight ahead with your natural relaxed expression. We'll capture your baseline in a few seconds.
vision.calibration.step3.title: Step 3: Test your gestures
vision.calibration.step3.desc: Try each gesture and adjust the sensitivity until it feels natural.
vision.calibration.step3.test_jaw_left: Shift your jaw to the left
vision.calibration.step3.test_jaw_right: Shift your jaw to the right
vision.mapping.title: Gesture Mapping
vision.mapping.desc: Configure what action each gesture triggers in games.
vision.mapping.head_disabled_hint: Switch head tracking to "Directional Keys" to configure these bindings.
vision.mapping.conflict_warning: Multiple gestures share the same key. The last one to fire will win.
play.panel.title: Control panel
play.panel.title_hint: Tweak face control without leaving the game
play.panel.dispatch_hint: When enabled, your gestures and movements will be sent to the game.
play.panel.no_config_hint: Configure your gestures in Settings to enable face control.
play.panel.unconfigured.title: Play hands-free
play.panel.unconfigured.description: Set up face control to play using gestures and head movements.
play.overlay.handshake.title: Waiting for the game
play.overlay.handshake.description: Establishing a secure connection with the embedded game.
play.overlay.loading.title: Loading face control
play.overlay.loading.description: Initializing MediaPipe and the camera. This may take a few seconds.
play.overlay.error.title: Something went wrong
play.overlay.error.description: Face control encountered a problem. Please try again.
play.overlay.timeout.title: Game not responding
play.overlay.timeout.description: The game took too long to connect. It may be down or the URL may be invalid.
play.preset_suggestion.text: This game suggests the ":preset" preset. Apply it?
developers.docs.integration_guide.title: Integration guide
developers.landing.hero.title: The identity that powers the entire Vout ecosystem
developers.landing.hero.subtitle: One login for your app. OAuth 2.1, PKCE, optional facial controls — and your users signed in within seconds.
developers.landing.features.heading: Why integrate with Vout
developers.landing.features.identity.des: One passport for every game and app in the ecosystem. No more duplicated signups.
developers.landing.features.fast.title: Integrate in minutes
developers.landing.features.fast.desc: Standard OAuth 2.1 with PKCE. Works with any SDK that speaks JWT.
developers.landing.features.privacy.desc: Local processing, no biometric storage. Users' data never leaves their device.
developers.landing.steps.heading: How to get started
developers.landing.steps.step_1.title: Create your account
developers.landing.steps.step_1.desc: Sign up for Vout for free with email or Google.
developers.landing.steps.step_2.title: Register your application
developers.landing.steps.step_2.desc: Declare name, URL, allowed origins and OAuth redirect URIs.
developers.landing.steps.step_3.title: Copy your credentials
developers.landing.steps.step_3.desc: Get your client_id and client_secret, generated by Passport.
developers.landing.steps.step_4.title: Integrate and ship
developers.landing.steps.step_4.desc: Follow the technical guide and launch "Sign in with Vout" in your app.
developers.landing.guides.heading: Technical documentation
developers.landing.guides.subheading: Guides designed to cut to the point: no filler, just copy-pasteable examples.
developers.landing.faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
developers.landing.faq.q1: Do I need approval to publish my app?
developers.landing.faq.a1: No. Any registered account can create apps from the dashboard. Clients are issued instantly.
developers.landing.faq.a2: user:read, user:email, games:read, games:write, and game:play. Combine them according to your app flow.
developers.landing.faq.q3: Can I use Vout without Passport on my backend?
developers.landing.faq.a3: Yes. Vout is a standard OAuth 2.1 server. Any JWT library (lcobucci/jwt, jose, etc.) validates tokens against our public key.
developers.landing.faq.q4: What if I lose the client_secret?
developers.landing.faq.a4: Regenerate it from the dashboard. The old secret is invalidated instantly; Vout never stores it in plain text.
developers.landing.final_cta.heading: Ready to integrate Vout?
developers.landing.final_cta.description: Create your first application in under a minute. No credit cards, no approvals.
developers.secret.title: Your application credentials
developers.secret.description: Save these values somewhere safe before closing this dialog.
developers.secret.warning_title: You won't see this secret again
developers.secret.warning_body: Vout stores the client_secret hashed. If you lose it you will need to regenerate it, which invalidates the previous one.
developers.form.name: Name
developers.form.app_url_hint: Where your project lives (homepage). It will appear on the public catalog page.
developers.form.allowed_origins_hint: Domains where Vout will accept hosting your games. Scheme + host only, no paths.
developers.form.allowed_origins_auto_hin: We auto-fill it from your website. Add more only if you have staging, a CDN, or other environments.
developers.form.redirect_uris_hint: Exact pages Vout returns the user to after a login. Each must start with one of your authorized domains.
developers.dashboard.index.title: My applications
developers.dashboard.index.heading: My applications
developers.dashboard.index.subheading: Apps you've registered on the Vout ecosystem.
developers.dashboard.index.empty.title: No applications yet
developers.dashboard.index.empty.descrip: Register your first app to obtain OAuth credentials and ship your Vout integration.
developers.dashboard.create.title: New application
developers.dashboard.create.heading: Register a new application
developers.dashboard.create.subheading: Provide the basics and get your credentials the moment you submit.
developers.dashboard.create.profile.head: Integration profile
developers.dashboard.create.profile.desc: Choose how your app connects to Vout.
developers.dashboard.create.profile.with: Your game will identify players with their Vout account («Sign in with Vout» button).
developers.dashboard.create.profile.clie: Your game runs inside an iframe and doesn't need to identify the player.
developers.dashboard.create.meta.heading: Project identity
developers.dashboard.create.meta.descrip: What players will see in the catalog.
developers.dashboard.create.origins.head: Where your app lives
developers.dashboard.create.origins.desc: Vout will only host games whose URL belongs to one of these domains.
developers.dashboard.create.oauth.descri: OAuth flow setup: where to send the user back after authentication and what kind of client you are.
developers.dashboard.create.help.heading: How your project fits
developers.dashboard.show.suspended.titl: This app has been suspended by an administrator
developers.dashboard.show.suspended.acti: This app is suspended. Actions are disabled until an administrator reactivates it.
developers.dashboard.show.overview.name: Name
developers.dashboard.show.credentials.de: Data your app uses to authenticate users with Vout.
developers.dashboard.show.credentials.se: The client_secret is only shown once
If you lost it, regenerate it and update your backend. The previous one is invalidated immediately.
developers.dashboard.show.credentials.re: The previous secret will stop working instantly. Every backend relying on it will need to be updated.
developers.dashboard.show.credentials.is: Your app has no active OAuth credentials. Issue a new pair to authenticate users again.
The associated OAuth client was revoked (usually after a moderation suspension). Issuing new credentials generates a fresh client_id and client_secret and leaves the old ones invalidated forever.
Resolve the suspension with the moderation team first. While it is active, no credentials can be issued.
Each redirect_uri must belong to one of your current allowed origins (:origins). If you need new domains, edit them first in the «Allowed origins» tab.
Tick this if your backend can keep the secret safe (server-side). If you are integrating from a SPA without a backend, leave it unchecked to use PKCE without a secret.
developers.dashboard.show.origins.descri: Update the authorized domains and OAuth redirect URIs. Changes apply immediately.
developers.dashboard.show.danger.heading: Danger zone
developers.dashboard.show.danger.pause.d: Keeps the app visible in your dashboard but disabled across the portal.
Flip the app back to active for the ecosystem.
Deletes the app and revokes its OAuth client along with every token. Cannot be undone.
You are about to delete ":slug" and every OAuth token issued to its users. Your integrations will stop working.
There is no trash. To reuse this app you'll have to create it again and update the client_id on your backend.
The text does not match the slug.
developers.games.index.title: My games
developers.games.index.heading: Games submitted to the catalog
developers.games.index.subheading: Submit your game for review and track its status at any time.
developers.games.index.empty.title: You haven't submitted any games yet
developers.games.index.empty.description: Link your game to one of your ecosystem apps and our team will review it.
developers.games.index.empty.needs_app: You first need an active app to link the game to.
developers.games.create.title: Submit game
developers.games.create.heading: Submit a game to the catalog
developers.games.create.subheading: Fill in your game's details. Once submitted, it enters the review queue.
developers.games.create.no_apps.title: You need an active app
developers.games.create.no_apps.descript: To link a game you must have at least one registered, active app in the portal.
developers.games.show.title: Game detail
developers.games.show.resubmit_hint: Saving changes to a rejected game puts it back in the review queue automatically.
developers.games.show.rejected.title: Your game was rejected
developers.games.show.rejected.body: Edit the points raised by our team and submit again.
developers.games.show.published.title: Game published
developers.games.show.published.body: This game is live in Vout's public catalog. You can keep updating it (description, cover, categories, embed, repo…) — changes go live instantly. The public URL does not change even if you rename the game, so shared links stay intact.
developers.games.show.app_suspended.body: The moderation team has suspended the app linked to this game. While that stands, the game cannot run or be modified. Check the app to see why and how to resolve it.
developers.games.show.app_paused.title: App «:app» is paused
developers.games.show.app_paused.body: You paused the linked app from your dashboard. The game will not load until you resume it.
developers.games.show.overview.heading: Overview
developers.games.show.overview.descripti: Public information of the game once approved.
developers.games.show.danger.heading: Danger zone
developers.games.show.danger.description: Deleting the game also removes its category and developer relations.
developers.games.show.danger.delete.desc: This cannot be undone. Published games can only be retired by the admin team.
developers.games.show.danger.delete.conf: You are about to delete ":slug" and all its relations.
developers.games.show.danger.delete.warn: If it was published it will disappear from the catalog immediately.
developers.games.show.danger.delete.mism: The text does not match the slug.
developers.games.show.danger.not_deletab: Published games can only be retired by the admin team.
developers.games.form.name.label: Game name
developers.games.form.description.label: Description
developers.games.form.description.placeh: Explain what the game is about, its goal and basic controls.
developers.games.form.description.hint: Between 20 and 2000 characters. Shown on the public page.
developers.games.form.app.label: Host app
developers.games.form.app.hint: Your game will inherit the authorized domains (and Vout login, if the app has it on).
developers.games.form.embed_url.label: Playable URL
developers.games.form.embed_url.hint: Exact page where your game lives. The domain is picked from your authorized list; you only write the path.
developers.games.form.embed_url.no_app_h: Select an app first to compose the URL.
developers.games.form.cover_image.label: Cover image URL
developers.games.form.release_date.label: Release date
developers.games.form.release_date.hint: Optional. Leave empty if not released yet.
developers.games.form.repo_url.label: Public repository
developers.games.form.repo_url.hint: Optional. GitHub/GitLab link if the game is open source.
developers.games.form.categories.label: Categories
developers.games.form.categories.hint: Pick between 1 and 5 categories that describe the game.
developers.games.form.developers.label: Studio and collaborators
developers.games.form.developers.hint: Optional. You already appear as the author because you are the submitter. Credit here the studio or any collaborators who also worked on the game. These are public entries maintained by the Vout team; if one is missing, reach out to support to add it.
developers.games.form.section.basics.des: Public name and description shown on the catalog page.
developers.games.form.section.app.title: Host app
developers.games.form.section.app.descri: The embed URL must fall under one of the selected app's allowed origins.
developers.games.form.section.meta.title: Extra metadata
developers.games.form.section.meta.descr: Cover, release date and repository — all optional but recommended.
developers.games.form.section.taxonomy.d: Helps users find your game in the catalog.
developers.games.form.help.heading: What you should know
developers.games.form.help.review.descri: Games enter the queue as pending. You can edit while they are in review or rejected.
developers.hints.app_url.label: More about Website
developers.hints.app_url.body: The public home of your project. We use it as the "visit website" link on the game card and as the starting point to auto-fill your authorized domains.
developers.hints.allowed_origins.label: More about Authorized domains
developers.hints.allowed_origins.body: Your app's security whitelist. Only from these domains can you register playable URLs, and only to them will Vout send tokens via postMessage. If you only have one website, one is enough.
developers.hints.redirect_uris.label: More about Return URLs
developers.hints.redirect_uris.body: When a player clicks "Sign in with Vout", Vout redirects them back here after authentication. They must start with one of your authorized domains.
developers.hints.embed_url.label: More about Playable URL
developers.hints.embed_url.body: The exact page loaded inside the portal's iframe. For security, it must belong to one of the authorized domains of the host app.
developers.flow.aria_label: Diagram of a game's flow in Vout
developers.flow.app.step1.title: Your website
developers.flow.app.step1.description: Your project lives on your own domain.
developers.flow.app.step2.title: Vout authorizes it
developers.flow.app.step2.description: You register your domains so Vout can host your games.
developers.flow.app.step3.title: Players play
developers.flow.app.step3.description: Your games appear in the catalog and load inside a secure iframe.
developers.flow.game.step1.title: Playable URL
developers.flow.game.step1.description: The exact page inside your app where the game lives.
developers.flow.game.step2.title: Embedded in Vout
developers.flow.game.step2.description: Vout loads it in an iframe and verifies the domain.
developers.flow.game.step3.title: Optional identity
developers.flow.game.step3.description: If your app has Vout login, it receives the player's token via postMessage.
developers.games.flash.submitted: Your game was submitted for review.
developers.form.client_type.heading: Does your app have its own backend?
developers.form.client_type.description: Determines how Vout authenticates your app when a player signs in.
developers.form.client_type.confidential: My app has its own server (Laravel, Node, Django, Rails…). Vout will issue a client_secret that stays safely on that backend.
developers.form.client_type.public.title: No, client-only
developers.form.client_type.public.descr: Pure SPA, mobile app or external backend (Firebase, Supabase). Vout will protect the flow with PKCE instead of a secret.
developers.form.client_type.note: If in doubt, leave "Yes, I have a backend". You can change it later by regenerating credentials.
developers.apps.suspended_blocked: This app is suspended. You cannot modify it until an administrator reactivates it.
developers.apps.credentials.not_applicab: This app does not use OAuth credentials. Switch its profile to «with IdP» to issue them.
developers.apps.credentials.already_issu: This app already has active credentials. Regenerate the secret from the Credentials tab if you need to rotate it.

admin.apps.show.actions.suspend.title: Suspend app
admin.apps.show.actions.suspend.descript: Immediately disable this app and revoke its OAuth credentials.
admin.apps.show.actions.suspend.confirm_: Suspending will permanently revoke the OAuth client. The owner will need to regenerate credentials after reactivation.
admin.apps.show.actions.suspend.warning_: All active access tokens for this app will stop working immediately. Users currently authenticated via this app will be logged out.
admin.apps.show.actions.suspend.remark_p: Describe why this app is being suspended (min. 10 characters)…
admin.apps.show.actions.reactivate.title: Reactivate app
admin.apps.show.actions.reactivate.descr: Remove the suspension and allow the owner to use this app again.
admin.apps.show.actions.reactivate.confi: The app will be re-enabled, but the owner must regenerate OAuth credentials from their dashboard.
admin.apps.show.actions.reactivate.note_: The previous OAuth client was permanently revoked during suspension. The developer must create new credentials.
admin.apps.show.actions.delete.title: Delete app
admin.apps.show.actions.delete.descripti: Permanently remove this app and all its data. This cannot be undone.
admin.apps.show.actions.delete.warning_b: The app, its OAuth credentials, and all associated data will be permanently deleted.

admin.developers.edit.description: Update the developer details. The slug will be regenerated from the new name.
admin.developers.edit.danger.title: Danger zone
admin.developers.edit.danger.delete.desc: Permanently delete this developer. This action cannot be undone.
admin.developers.edit.danger.delete.has_: This developer has :count associated games. Reassign them before deleting.
admin.developers.edit.danger.delete.conf: Type the slug :slug to confirm permanent deletion.
admin.developers.edit.danger.delete.warn: This will permanently delete the developer and remove it from all games.
admin.developers.has_games: This developer has associated games. Reassign them before deleting.
admin.developers.edit.ownership.title: Owner
admin.developers.edit.ownership.no_owner: This entry is manual — no user is maintaining it from the portal.
admin.developers.edit.ownership.reassign: Change or clear the user who maintains this entry. The action is recorded in the audit log.
Pick a new owner or leave the entry as manual (no owner). Only users who haven’t claimed any other entry are listed.
admin.developers.owner_cleared: Owner cleared. The entry is now manual.
developers.profile.edit.title: My public profile
developers.profile.edit.description: This is how the Vout catalog will show you. Changes are immediate.
developers.profile.claim.title: Claim your profile
developers.profile.claim.description: Create your public profile so Vout can credit you automatically on every game you submit.
developers.profile.form.section.identity: Your name is how the catalog will credit you. You can change it later.
developers.profile.form.section.links.de: Optional. Make your profile shine in the public catalog.
developers.profile.form.name.label: Public name
developers.profile.form.name.hint: Must be unique across the Vout ecosystem. Between 2 and 150 characters.
developers.profile.form.bio.label: Bio
developers.profile.form.bio.placeholder: Tell the world who you are and what games you make…
developers.profile.form.logo_url.label: Logo URL
developers.profile.form.logo_url.hint: Optional. HTTPS, square image; it will be shown as your avatar.
developers.profile.meta.title: Details
developers.profile.admin_hint.title: Note
developers.profile.admin_hint.descriptio: The Vout team can reassign ownership in exceptional cases (e.g. impersonation or corporate accounts). Your changes are recorded.
developers.profile.preview.title: Preview
developers.profile.preview.description: This is how your profile will look in the catalog.
developers.profile.preview.auto_attach: Will be auto-attached to every game you submit.
developers.profile.preview.catalog_hint: Published in the catalog with your name and links.
developers.profile.preview.claim_hint: Once claimed, your profile will appear on every game you’ve already submitted.
developers.games.form.developers.own_pro: Your profile :name will be auto-attached — you don’t need to tick it here.
developers.games.form.developers.claim_h: You don’t have a public profile yet. Claim yours so the catalog can credit you on your games.
admin.dashboard.title: Admin dashboard
admin.dashboard.description: The pulse of the ecosystem at a glance — counts, health and recent activity.
admin.dashboard.metrics.games_pending_hi: Games submitted by developers waiting for your decision.
admin.dashboard.activity.title: Recent activity
admin.dashboard.activity.empty: No administrative actions have been recorded yet.
admin.audit.index.title: Audit log
admin.audit.index.description: Every critical admin action is logged here for traceability and postmortems.
admin.audit.index.empty: The audit log is empty — no critical action has been recorded yet.
admin.audit.index.empty_filtered: No entries match the current filters.
admin.audit.filters.heading: Filters
